title: Special Programs: Gifted and Talented Students
description: Conroe ISD's approach to identifying and serving gifted and talented students is outlined here, covering how students are selected for these programs and what kinds of services and instruction they receive. The policy ensures the district meets state requirements for supporting students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability or creative talent. Parents, students, and educators can refer to this policy to understand their rights and the district's responsibilities in providing advanced learning opportunities.

timeline_local.summary: This update to Conroe ISD's Gifted and Talented policy (EHBB Local) renames the "selection" committee to "placement" committee throughout the document, replaces vague exit-trigger language with a clearer student-needs standard, and substantially rewrites the Funding section to require the Superintendent to develop TEA-aligned administrative procedures and mandates that 100% of state G/T funds be spent on G/T services or program enhancement.
changes_added: identification (replacing 'assessment' in the phrase describing the awareness session overview of identification/assessment procedures)
placement (replacing 'selection' in 'placement committee' — used throughout: Selection section, Interdistrict section, Exit Provisions section, Appeals section)
is not meeting the student's educational needs (replacing 'it is in the best interest of the student to exit')
services in (replacing 'selection for or exit from' in Appeals section)
Superintendent or designee shall develop administrative procedures to account for the expenditure of the (replacing 'District's' in Funding section)
allotment in alignment with the Texas Education Agency's financial compliance guidance. (added after 'gifted and talented' in Funding section)

One hundred percent of state funds allocated for gifted and talented education shall be spent on providing gifted and talented services or enhancing the District's gifted and talented program. (new sentence added to Funding section)
changes_removed: assessment (in the phrase describing the awareness session overview of 'identification assessment procedures')
selection (replaced by 'placement' throughout — in Selection section, Interdistrict section, Exit Provisions section, Appeals section)
it is in the best interest of the student to exit (in Exit Provisions section)
selection for or exit from (in Appeals section)
District's (at the start of the Funding section sentence)
program shall address effective use of funds for programs and services consistent with the standards in the state plan for gifted and talented students. (removed from Funding section)
timeline_local.notes: Red underlined and red strikethrough text was clearly distinguishable throughout pages 2–4. The committee rename from 'selection' to 'placement' occurs in multiple locations and is treated as a single recurring change. The Funding section had the most substantive rewrites. Page 5 is a date/version footer page with no policy text changes.

timeline_local.summary: This update to Conroe ISD's Gifted and Talented policy (EHBB Local) replaces "selection" with "placement" when referring to the program committee throughout the document, updates terminology in the screening/identification process, revises the exit provisions language to focus on student educational needs, and significantly rewrites the Funding section to align expenditures with TEA financial compliance guidance and require that 100% of state GT funds be spent on GT services or program enhancement.

placement (replacing 'selection' in the Selection section committee reference)
placement (replacing 'selection' in the Interdistrict Transfer Students committee reference)
placement (replacing 'selection' in the Exit Provisions committee reference)
is not meeting the student's educational needs (replacing 'it is in the best interest of the student to exit')
placement (replacing 'selection' in the Appeals section — committee reference)

placement (replacing 'selection' in the second Appeals committee reference)
Superintendent or designee shall develop administrative procedures to account for the expenditure of the (replacing 'District's')
allotment in alignment with the Texas Education Agency's financial compliance guidance. (replacing the old program standards language)
One hundred percent of state funds allocated for gifted and talented education shall be spent on providing gifted and talented services or enhancing the District's gifted and talented program.
changes_removed: assessment (in the awareness session sentence, replaced by 'identification')
selection (in the Selection section committee reference, replaced by 'placement')
selection (in the Interdistrict Transfer Students committee reference, replaced by 'placement')
selection (in the Exit Provisions committee reference, replaced by 'placement')

program shall address effective use of funds for programs and services consistent with the standards in the state plan for gifted and talented students.
timeline_local.notes: Red underlined and red strikethrough text was clearly distinguishable throughout the document. The primary changes are: (1) consistent terminology shift from 'selection committee' to 'placement committee'; (2) 'assessment' to 'identification' in one instance; (3) exit provisions language update; and (4) a substantive rewrite of the Funding section adding a new sentence requiring 100% of state GT funds be spent on GT services/program enhancement.

description_local: This policy sets the rules for how a school district identifies, serves, and supports students in its gifted and talented program, including how students can enter or exit the program and how families can appeal placement decisions. It applies to students who are nominated or selected for gifted and talented services, as well as their parents and the staff who work with them. Parents should be aware of this policy because it outlines their rights to challenge decisions about their child's participation in the program and explains how the district funds and maintains these specialized services.
